summary: ROI ledger promotion (RML) implementation reviewed end-to-end: central-ledger promotion is computed and staged in the same temp-worktree commit as canonical audit persistence (never a follow-up commit), dedupe by entry_key is proven against real pushed git bytes, corrupt/malformed central ledgers fail closed without overwrite, unclassified_count is always explicit and never coerced to zero in both JSON and text render paths, --gate-roi is opt-in and backward compatible, CLI help text matches actual cli.js flag wiring, and the new docs/management/roi-ledger/ledger.json seed matches serializeCentralGateOutcomeLedger's shape. All 11 targeted unit+integration tests pass and are substantive - the integration tests drive the real production computeCentralLedgerPromotion function through actual git worktree/commit/push cycles, including a same-story two-merge dedupe scenario.

findings.detail: test/gate-outcome-ledger-promotion-integration.test.js drives the real computeCentralLedgerPromotion() but the staging/commit sequence around it is a hand-written mirror of persistCanonicalAuditToBase, not a call into that function. Future drift would not be caught by this test.
Local per-story ledger is never pruned after successful promotion; safe due to entry_key dedupe but grows unbounded. Design note.
